method: Cross-site sign-frequency concordance: mean pairwise Spearman rank correlation of per-site sign-frequency vectors over the union sign inventory; within-site sign-label shuffle null (full permutation over the union inventory per site, >=1000 draws, one-sided p). LB positive control (seeded pseudo-site partition) gates the verdict. Leave-one-site-out + per-site mean correlation for outlier structure.
non_circular: Signs are ANONYMOUS ids (the corpus sign tokens as written). No phonetics, no sound, no meaning, no reading, no cognacy is assigned or used. All statistics are L2/L3 distributional sign-usage only (frequency-rank concordance). LB is a positive-control benchmark ONLY and never enters the LA verdict except to gate the machinery. The verdict is a mechanical consequence of the frozen rule + the PC gate; the operator never adjudicates.

positive_control.lb_pseudo_site_partition: SEEDED shuffle of DĀMOS wordforms into 5 chunks of 1200 wordforms each (DĀMOS has no site metadata)

outlier.note: Lowest per-site mean correlation is Khania (0.518); it is NOT >2 SD below the across-site mean (0.593) and the gap to the next site (0.040) is well under 1 SD of the per-site values (~0.054). No site qualifies as a low outlier.

leave_one_out.note: Every leave-one-out concordance (0.581-0.634) remains far above the null mean (~0.00, null std ~0.012). Concordance is NOT driven by a single pair; it survives removal of any one site.

key_findings: The 8 qualifying Linear A sites (>=100 sign tokens) share a strong common sign-FREQUENCY fingerprint: mean pairwise Spearman rank correlation of per-site sign-frequency vectors = 0.605 over a 234-sign union inventory, vs a within-site sign-label-shuffle null centred at ~0.00 (std ~0.012); one-sided p = 0.0 (1000 draws).

This is the symmetric counterpart to E032: WORD-FORMS are site-local, but the SIGN-INVENTORY usage profile is shared across sites — the signs that are frequent at one site are frequent everywhere. This is a writing-system-level (syllabary-usage) regularity, not a word-form regularity.
The effect is NOT driven by a single pair or a single site: every leave-one-site-out concordance stays in 0.581-0.634, far above the null, so the fingerprint is supported by all 8 sites jointly.

No site is a low outlier: per-site mean correlation to the others ranges 0.518 (Khania) to 0.674 (Knossos); the lowest (Khania) is within 2 SD of the across-site mean and shows no clear gap to the next site. There is no dialect-like divergent site in this (anonymous, frequency-only) view.

Positive control PASSED: LB (one shared script) seeded into 5 pseudo-sites gives concordance 0.978 (detect p = 0.0), and independent random-permutation pseudo-sites (no shared fingerprint) are rejected at rate 0.10 (<=0.10 across 20 sets), so the machinery is informative.
Haghia Triada dominates the token count (3054 of ~5078), yet removing it leaves concordance at 0.619 — the fingerprint is not an artefact of HT's sample size.
successor_hypotheses: H1 (L2/L3): The shared fingerprint is dominated by a small RANK-STABLE core of high-frequency signs — test whether the top-k (e.g. k=10) signs alone reproduce the concordance, and whether low-frequency signs are noisier across sites.

H2 (L2/L3): Decompose concordance by sign RANK BAND (head vs mid vs tail of the frequency distribution) to see whether the shared fingerprint is a Zipf-shape artefact (common signs common everywhere) vs sign-IDENTITY-specific alignment.

H3 (L2/L3): Test whether the small/underpowered sites (Syme, Tylissos, Malia, 69-88 tokens) JOIN the fingerprint when pooled or when a lower token threshold is used — is the fingerprint a Crete-wide or a high-documentation-site phenomenon?

H4 (L2/L3): Cross-check the sign-frequency fingerprint against the E032 site-local WORD-FORM result: do sites share sign USAGE but diverge in sign COMBINATION (bigram/word-form) statistics? This would localise the divergence to combinatorics, not inventory.

H5 (L2/L3, outlier hunt): With more tokens per small site, re-test for a divergent site; Khania is the lowest-correlating site here and is the natural candidate to monitor for dialect-like divergence as data grows.

H6 (L2/L3): Compare the LA sign-frequency fingerprint shape (rank-frequency curve) to LB's, anonymously, to ask whether the two scripts share a usage-shape regularity beyond the shared inventory already known from the anchor work.
